packs.name: BSL Quality
packs.what_it_does: Groups analyzers and quality gates for BSL codebases.
packs.first_smoke_check: Confirm the analyzer or plugin binary starts and can see a BSL project.
packs.next_scenario: Run diagnostics first, then connect findings to Explain Module or a review workflow.

packs.name: Testing
packs.what_it_does: Collects scripting and test execution tools used around 1C projects.
packs.first_smoke_check: Verify the runtime or runner answers with a version/help command before wiring test flows.
packs.next_scenario: Move from basic runtime validation into scripted checks, BDD, or xUnit-style regression runs.

packs.name: Review
packs.what_it_does: Tracks review-oriented tooling for AI-assisted code inspection and merge feedback.
packs.first_smoke_check: Verify the CLI or action entrypoint responds before connecting it to CI.
packs.next_scenario: Run a review pass on a small change set and compare it with the workbench's own prompts and rules.

packs.name: Prompt Gallery
packs.what_it_does: Exposes workbench prompt contracts as callable MCP tools instead of manual markdown snippets.
packs.first_smoke_check: Verify the prompt-gallery MCP server registers generic tools and one tool per prompt file.
packs.next_scenario: Call a prompt tool such as prompt_explain_module with task/context and then use code-index evidence to answer.

packs.name: 1C Skills
packs.what_it_does: Exposes read-only 1C metadata, form, role, SKD, MXL, and validation tools as MCP calls over the local source mirror.
packs.first_smoke_check: Run the skills bridge check and confirm all read-only tools register.
packs.next_scenario: Run meta_info on an object, then inspect its form, roles, or SKD artifacts without leaving the local source mirror.

packs.name: Live Configuration Ops
packs.what_it_does: Starts Phase B import/export operations around live 1C infobases without making live mode part of the default read-only stack.
packs.first_smoke_check: Verify the MCP bridge loads and ibcmd is either found or reported as optional missing.
packs.next_scenario: Dry-run ibcmd export for a pilot infobase, then export XML into generated/ibcmd-export and feed it into indexing.

packs.name: VS Code Cockpit
packs.what_it_does: Captures editor-side operator experiences worth mirroring in the workbench.
packs.first_smoke_check: Confirm the extension or command surface loads in VS Code/Cursor.
packs.next_scenario: Use the UI shape as a reference for guided actions, test trees, and metadata navigation.

packs.name: Reference
packs.what_it_does: Holds market and UX references that inform positioning but are not operational dependencies.
packs.first_smoke_check: Verify the reference is reachable and the product claims are attributable.
packs.next_scenario: Use it to sharpen product messaging, pack boundaries, and future workflow decisions.

tools.name: BSL Language Server
tools.why: Core static analysis layer for BSL and OneScript. It is highly relevant to explain, fix, and quality flows, but remains experimental in this workbench until a local check script exists.

smoke_check.expected: Help or command list appears without a JVM/runtime error.

tools.next_scenario: Add a workbench check command, then connect diagnostics output to Explain Module and curated prompt flows.

tools.name: SonarQube BSL Community Plugin
tools.why: Useful for team-level quality gates around BSL code, but it depends on SonarQube setup and broader environment choices. Enterprise Sonar usage is optional, not assumed.

smoke_check.expected: Scanner help opens, confirming the analysis entrypoint is available before any BSL project wiring.

tools.next_scenario: Document a minimal sample project and quality gate path before elevating beyond experimental.

tools.name: OneScript
tools.why: Important automation runtime for many 1C workflows, but Windows setup and downstream package expectations still need a tighter workbench recipe.

smoke_check.expected: The runtime prints its version and exits cleanly.

tools.next_scenario: Use it as the runtime base for scripted checks and for tools such as vanessa-runner.

tools.name: vanessa-runner
tools.why: Strong candidate for orchestrating test and build operations around 1C, but versioning is a real issue: 2.x LTS remains safer while 3.x introduces breaking changes.

smoke_check.expected: The runner prints available commands without requiring a project execution context.

tools.next_scenario: Add a documented profile for 2.x LTS first, then evaluate when a 3.x path is stable enough for the workbench.

tools.name: Vanessa Automation
tools.why: High-value BDD testing tool for 1C, but Phase A does not yet define a repeatable Windows smoke path inside the workbench without wider 1C test-base setup assumptions.

smoke_check.command: Open the official docs and release channel to confirm the current package line before any local rollout.
smoke_check.expected: Operator can identify the current distribution and prerequisites before deeper test-environment work.

tools.next_scenario: Phase B can define a test-base recipe and then promote it to experimental with a real smoke command.

tools.name: YAxUnit
tools.why: Relevant for modern 1C testing, but the workbench does not yet expose a standardized launch path or example project that would make the first check operational instead of aspirational.

smoke_check.command: Review the official repository and releases page to pin the intended distribution before adding a local test recipe.
smoke_check.expected: Operator can identify the supported package and release source for a future scripted check.

tools.next_scenario: Phase B can add a minimal fixture project and a documented launch path, then reclassify if the smoke flow becomes repeatable.

tools.name: ai-review
tools.why: Useful reference for CI-driven AI review workflows, but it is not 1C-specific and still needs curation before it fits the workbench's operator model.

smoke_check.expected: CLI help or entrypoint instructions appear, confirming the review surface exists.

tools.next_scenario: Compare its CI review model with the workbench's Prompt Gallery and path-scoped AI rules before integrating any review recipe.

tools.name: Prompt Gallery MCP
tools.why: The local prompt gallery was not callable through MCP. This server registers generic prompt discovery tools and one callable tool per markdown prompt.

smoke_check.expected: Prompt gallery loads, prompt count matches prompts/*.md, and prompt_explain_module renders caller context.

tools.next_scenario: Call prompt_gallery_list, then call a specific prompt tool with task and context from code-index-mcp.

tools.name: ibcmd Bridge
tools.why: Most direct Phase B path for vendor-supported configuration export/import, XML export comparison, indexing refresh, and EDT handoff. It is optional, disabled by default, and write operations are gated.

smoke_check.expected: Bridge imports, registers 6 MCP tools, dry-runs export/index, compares a fixture directory, and reports ibcmd presence or optional missing status.

tools.next_scenario: Run ibcmd_export_and_index with dry_run=true for a pilot infobase, then export to generated/ibcmd-export, index the result, and compare it with the previous export.

tools.name: Skills Bridge (cc-1c-skills)
tools.why: MCP-обёртка для 70+ Claude Code skills от Nikolay-Shirokov. Добавляет read-only интроспекцию метаданных 1С (объекты, формы, СКД, роли, подсистемы, конфигурации) и валидацию XML-структур.

smoke_check.expected: FastMCP help or server start message.

tools.next_scenario: Run meta_info on a catalog object, then form_info on its form, then skd_info on a report template.

tools.name: 1C: Platform Tools
tools.why: Strong cockpit and workflow reference for VS Code/Cursor, especially around command surfaces, navigation, test trees, and AI adjacency. Phase A treats it as a UX reference, not a dependency promise.

smoke_check.command: Install the extension in VS Code/Cursor and confirm the command palette shows 1C: Platform Tools commands.
smoke_check.expected: The extension loads and exposes its command surface.

tools.next_scenario: Borrow cockpit patterns for future workbench actions without coupling the workbench to the extension lifecycle.

tools.name: Confaster
tools.why: Relevant market reference: an AI agent inside 1C Configurator with an MCP claim. It remains reference-only because the distribution is closed-source and no public repository is available for trust and integration review.

smoke_check.command: Open the product site and verify the current feature claims, packaging, and download path.
smoke_check.expected: Operator can confirm that the reference still exists and what it claims to offer.

tools.next_scenario: Use it for competitive positioning and UX language, not as a dependency or code source.
